name: Product Launch Go-To-Market
description: Cross-functional team plans the go-to-market for a new AI-powered product, simulating market reception across segments.
seed_text:
Intercom is preparing to launch 'Fin Workflows' — an AI-powered workflow automation layer that lets support teams build no-code automation chains combining Fin AI resolution, human handoff rules, and proactive outreach triggers. Launch is planned for 6 weeks out.

Product details:
- Fin Workflows is available as an add-on ($49/seat/month) for Pro and Premium customers
- Key capabilities: auto-triage by intent + sentiment, conditional escalation paths, proactive messaging triggers based on usage patterns, and SLA-aware routing
- Beta results from 23 customers: 34% reduction in first-response time, 22% increase in CSAT, 18% reduction in ticket volume through proactive deflection
- Competitive landscape: Zendesk just launched a similar feature (Zendesk AI Agents) but it requires their Suite Enterprise plan ($150+/agent/month)

GTM challenges identified:
1. Positioning: Is this an 'AI product' or a 'workflow product'? The naming matters for buyer persona targeting
2. Enablement gap: Only 40% of AEs can confidently demo the existing Fin product — adding Workflows increases complexity
3. Packaging: Should Workflows be bundled into Premium or kept as a paid add-on to drive expansion revenue?
4. Launch timing: Dreamforce is in 4 weeks — do we launch before, during, or after to maximize press coverage?
5. Customer migration: 180 customers on legacy Custom Bots need a migration path to Fin Workflows

The simulation should model how target buyer personas receive the product positioning, which GTM motions (PLG vs sales-led vs hybrid) drive fastest adoption, and how competitive dynamics shape messaging effectiveness.
expected_outputs: Positioning resonance by persona and segment
GTM motion effectiveness comparison (PLG vs sales-led vs hybrid)
Pricing sensitivity analysis for add-on vs bundle
Competitive differentiation strength vs Zendesk AI Agents
Adoption curve prediction by customer segment
